- glibc_internal_abi.rs (102 call sites)

- set_abi_errno adoptions: 104 in glibc_internal_abi.rs

 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-pn6c.3","title":"Investigate and fix the 3 pre-existing pow errno test failures","description":"Three tests have been failing throughout all audit rounds:
- pow_overflow_sets_range_errno
- pow_underflow_sets_range_errno
- pow_zero_negative_sets_range_errno

All expect errno == ERANGE after pow() calls, but errno remains 0. The pow() function correctly calls set_range_errno() which calls set_abi_errno(libc::ERANGE). The test reads errno via __errno_location().

HYPOTHESIS: In test mode, something between set_abi_errno and the test's abi_errno() read is clearing errno. Possible causes:
1. runtime_policy::observe() or decide() inadvertently calls something that sets errno
2. The thread-local errno in test mode is different from the one used by set_abi_errno
3. A Rust standard library operation between the pow call and the assertion resets errno

DEBUG APPROACH: Add eprintln! tracing to set_abi_errno and __errno_location in test mode to verify they use the same TLS slot.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:15.268801545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:27:11.456442127Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:27:11.455469646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pn6c.3","depends_on_id":"bd-pn6c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:15.268801545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host).  {"id":"bd-xxyb","title":"Investigate Python startup perf regression in LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (51/7/6)","description":"## Problem
LD_PRELOAD smoke suite regressed from 58/0/6 to 51/7/6. All 7 failures are Python-related with failure_signature=startup_perf_regression. Correctness is fine (parity_pass=true) — only startup latency exceeds the perf gate threshold.

## Failing Cases
- strict: python3_print, stress_python_{1..5}
- hardened: stress_python_5

## Probable Cause
Recent changes to signal handling and malloc that added overhead:
1. signal_abi.rs: Signal trampoline installation, SA_RESTORER marshalling, deferred delivery critical sections
2. malloc_abi.rs: Signal critical section entry/exit around every malloc/calloc/free/realloc call
3. host_resolve.rs: Streaming /proc/self/maps parser changes

Python startup is particularly sensitive because it calls sigaction dozens of times during interpreter init, and does heavy allocation.

## Investigation Steps
1. Profile Python startup under LD_PRELOAD to identify the hotspot
2. Check if the malloc signal critical section is the bottleneck (atomic ops on every alloc)
3. Check if sigaction trampoline installation overhead is significant
4. Consider: make signal critical sections opt-in (only when FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened)

## Acceptance Criteria
Smoke suite returns to 58/0/6 (or 57/0/7 with the new skipped binary). Python startup perf within threshold.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:55.100768393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T00:24:27.961421919Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T00:23:57.951709506Z","close_reason":"Smoke gate back to 58/0/6; strict+hardened Python cases green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["malloc","performance","regression","signal"],"comments":[{"id":1367,"issue_id":"bd-xxyb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Investigation finding: strace -c output is garbled under LD_PRELOAD because our printf engine does NOT support POSIX positional format specifiers (%n$s, %n$d). strace uses these extensively for column-aligned output. ### Track B (bd-olgi): dlinfo — 1 function [P2]

### Track C (bd-osb8 -> bd-ta3b, bd-kcbm): gshadow — 8 functions [P3]
Location: pwd_abi.rs:969-1040. Currently return null/ENOENT. Should parse /etc/gshadow.

### Track D (bd-9dq5): gai_* async DNS — 3 functions [P3]
Location: unistd_abi.rs:17404-17423. Currently return ENOSYS incorrectly. Should return correct POSIX error codes for synchronous-only resolver.

## Execution Strategy
Ordered by impact-to-effort: A (30min) > B (45min) > D (20min) > C (90min).

## Non-goals
~100 intentional ENOSYS/no-op stubs for deprecated/removed/BSD-only syscalls, profiling hooks, and legacy RPC are NOT in scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
All 12 stub functions replaced with real implementations or correct error returns. All unit tests pass. All e2e scripts pass. Workspace clippy clean. ### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`

- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.

- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.

 {"id":"bd-zh1y","title":"EPIC: Eliminate all remaining host delegations (resolve_host_symbol_raw to 0)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate every remaining resolve_host_symbol_raw call across the ABI crate (except host_resolve.rs which IS the resolver infrastructure), bringing FrankenLibC to zero host libc runtime dependencies for its classified symbol surface.

## Background
As of commit d974b43, 64 host delegations were eliminated (57 from unistd_abi.rs, 6 from rpc_abi.rs, 1 from glibc_internal_abi.rs). Code audit reveals 31 remaining calls across 7 files (excluding host_resolve.rs):

### Remaining by file (31 total, verified by code audit)
| File | Calls | Unique Symbols | Category |
|------|-------|---------------|----------|
| io_internal_abi.rs | 7 | 3 (_IO_list_all, _IO_file_jumps, _IO_wfile_jumps) | glibc stream internals |
| setjmp_abi.rs | 6 | 4 (setjmp, _setjmp, __sigsetjmp, longjmp/siglongjmp) | register save/restore |
| stdio_abi.rs | 5 | 5 (stdin/stdout/stderr, setvbuf, host fn cache) | host FILE* bootstrap |
| pthread_abi.rs | 5 | 5 (getattr_np, cond_clockwait, timedjoin, tryjoin, clockjoin) | pthread extensions |
| dlfcn_abi.rs | 4 | 3 (dlopen x2, dlsym, dlclose) | dynamic loader |
| stdlib_abi.rs | 3 | 3 (setenv, unsetenv, putenv) | environ array |
| resolv_abi.rs | 2 | 2 (getaddrinfo, freeaddrinfo) | DNS resolution |

Note: host_resolve.rs has 3 calls but is the resolver infrastructure itself — not a promotion target.

### Two categories of remaining delegations
**Category A — Intentional Interpose Delegations (18 calls):**
These delegate to host ONLY for objects the host created (non-managed). Native paths already work for FrankenLibC-managed objects. Correct for LD_PRELOAD mode; dead code in replace mode.
- pthread_abi.rs: 5 (all conditional on !is_managed_*)
- dlfcn_abi.rs: 3 (dlsym/dlclose for host-opened handles)
- stdio_abi.rs: 2 (setvbuf + host fn cache for non-registry FILE*)
- stdlib_abi.rs: 3 (prefer host to avoid environ ownership conflict)

**Category B — Genuinely Missing Native Implementations (13 calls):**
These need new code — the host delegation is the primary/only path.
- setjmp_abi.rs: 6 (UNCONDITIONAL — always delegates, needs assembly)
- io_internal_abi.rs: 7 (bootstrap host glibc _IO_* structures)
- stdio_abi.rs: 3 (stdin/stdout/stderr global resolution)
- resolv_abi.rs: 1 (getaddrinfo DNS fallback — /etc/hosts already native)
- dlfcn_abi.rs: 1 (dlopen bootstrap passthrough)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw calls in ABI crate except host_resolve.rs
- All existing tests pass (156 lib + 113 unistd + 88 stdio + 35 rpc + 21 dlfcn + 21 setjmp + 163 pthread + all others)
- Full workspace clippy clean
- Each sub-bead includes: unit tests, e2e script, structured logging, verification commands
- Category A beads may be deferred to replace-mode milestone if interpose behavior is correct","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:41:10.103151134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:09.722735542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:09.722366513Z","close_reason":"ALL 7 children closed: bd-zh1y.1 (pthread extensions), bd-zh1y.2 (setjmp/longjmp asm), bd-zh1y.3 (native FILE/vtable/registry), bd-zh1y.4 (stdio globals), bd-zh1y.5 (dlfcn classification), bd-zh1y.6 (environ ownership), bd-zh1y.7 (getaddrinfo DNS).
